title: Oldenlandia effusa
taxonomy: Oldenlandia effusa belongs to the family Rubiaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Oldenlandia, which is a group of herbs and shrubs native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Oldenlandia is part of the tribe Spermaeae, which includes about 20 genera. Oldenlandia effusa is native to tropical Asia, including countries such as China, India, and Southeast Asia. Its classification is also supported by the APG III system, which places it in the clade Asterids.
uses: Oldenlandia effusa is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is reported to have potential in treating various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In some parts of the world, it is used as a treatment for respiratory problems and as a diuretic. The plant's leaves and roots are typically used in herbal remedies. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and potential side effects of using Oldenlandia effusa for medicinal purposes.

intro: Oldenlandia effusa, also known as iswara or false buttonweed, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia effusa include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Oldenlandia effusa is often used to prepare herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powder, which are used to treat various health conditions.
